name: Nano
description: My grandmother, who has tended motia plants for over fifty years. Her hands know the flower in ways no text can describe.
quote: The motia doesn't ask you to love it. You just do. It is in the morning, in the evening, in every prayer. It is the house.

name: Ammi
description: My mother, who carried the scent of motia from her childhood home into ours. She speaks of the flower as something inseparable from the women who tend it.
quote: When I smell motia, I am not here. I am in my mother's garden, and I am twelve years old, and the world is small and warm.

name: Khalajan
description: My grandmother's sister, who remembers the motia fields of their childhood village. Her stories reach further back than anyone else's.
quote: We did not buy motia. It grew. Everywhere it grew. And every girl knew how to make a gajra before she knew how to read.
